title: The Sea-Wolf
author: Jack London
content: “The individual is a small thing, and the total of the individuals is a small thing, and the total of the individuals is a bigger thing.”
action: Assess your position in the collective.

title: Life on the Mississippi
author: Mark Twain
content: “The face of the water, in time, became a wonderful book—a book that was a dead language to the uneducated passenger.”
action: Learn the language of your environment.

title: Journal (Entry: August 1851)
author: Henry David Thoreau
content: “The question is not what you look at, but what you see.”

title: To Build a Fire
author: Jack London
content: “The animal knew more than he did about the temperatures.”

title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court
author: Mark Twain
content: “Training is everything. The peach was once a bitter almond; cauliflower is nothing but cabbage with a college education.”
